titleStmt.title: Letter from Thomas O'Donnell to Augustine Birrell, 9 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas O'Donnell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Thomas O' Donnell (1871-1943), an Irish member of parliament, to Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), the Chief Secretary for Ireland. Thomas O'Donnell was an Irish Nationalist MP who held his seat for West Kerry from 1900- 1918. Augustine Birrell was the Chief Secretary for Ireland from 1907 until 1916 when he resigned after his failure to prevent and manage the Easter Rising. The letter is a request for an interview with Birrell over the Easter week.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/6893/9/3

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
J.Q.
Have the Justice
any knowledge of
this case. Early
report please
M.N. 9/4 6-4-16 Dear Mr. Birell, The Killorglin
Town Tenants are most
anxious to have an
interview with you during
the Easter recess if you
are then in Dublin. I
trust you will find it
convenient to arrange as
the matter is urgent &
most important. V. Sincy Yours Thos O'Donnell
